title: Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises, First Series
summary: aha2688 is the repository copy of the University of Michigan OCR/Level-1 TEI representation of selected Middle English source texts from Richard Morris's Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises, First Series (EETS Original Series 29 and 34, completed 1868). Morris compiled twenty-nine numbered print containers from Lambeth Palace Library MS 487, Cotton MSS Nero A XIV and Vespasian A XXII, Bodleian MS Bodley 34, Royal MS 17 A XXVII, Cotton MS Titus D XVIII, and Egerton MS 613. George Parker supplied the Bodley transcript and Royal/Titus collations behind Morris's Sawles Warde text. The U-M encoding omits Morris's introduction, facing modern-English translations, much apparatus, Appendix II, and back matter; it includes Appendix I and Appendix III. It also merges printed XX and XXI into XML division N=20 and represents the complete Nero Orison only partially, beginning at Morris p. 201 / Nero f. 124v after omitting its heading and p. 200 / f. 124r opening. The retained 710-page Internet Archive PDF is complete as a scan carrier, but only PDF pp. 5–398 reproduce the target First Series; a separately bound Second Series begins at PDF p. 399 and is not part of the target edition. Egerton MS 613 has a complete 197-canvas public IIIF object, mapped to the target at ff. 7r–12v / canvases 39–50, but it is not bulk-cached because the manifest limits its public-domain statement geographically and BL terms require caution about systematic extraction. No complete public facsimile was verified for the other six manuscripts. An exact official April 2026 U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A PDF is cached separately as supporting United States rights evidence; it contains no aha2688 text or images and is not part of the substantive transmission chain.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2688
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 403,411 bytes and identifies the source item as AHA2688.0001.001.
The repository representation inherits U-M OCR and selection boundaries; it is not an independent manuscript transcription.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2688.0001.001
entities.description: A public uncorrected-OCR, TEI-in-Libraries Level-1 representation of Morris's title page and selected Middle English source-text pages. It links 394 page images but omits the front matter, modern-English translations, Appendix II, and back matter.

entities.label: Richard Morris, Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises, First Series (1868)
entities.description: Morris's edited anthology of Middle English source texts with introduction, facing modern-English translations, notes, glossary, and appendices. Issued in two parts in 1867–1868 as EETS Original Series 29 and 34, it arranges the numbered texts in manuscript order by source group rather than presenting one medieval codex or one medieval work.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Morris, ed., Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises (Sawles Warde, and þe Wohunge of Ure Lauerd: Ureisuns of Ure Louerd and of Ure Lefdi, &c.) of the Twelfth and Thirteenth Centuries, First Series, EETS Original Series 29 and 34 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1868), 2 vols. in 1, lxiii + 330 pp.
bibliographic.title: Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises
bibliographic.volume: First Series; EETS Original Series 29 and 34
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive 710-page bound scan containing Morris's First and Second Series
entities.description: A complete 710-page scan carrier. The target First Series occupies PDF pp. 5–398; separately bound Second Series preliminaries begin at PDF p. 399 and its title page is PDF p. 405. The Second Series is a distinct non-target binding component, not part of the First Series edition represented by aha2688.

notes: First Series is complete within PDF pp. 5–398.
The carrier continues with a separately bound Second Series from PDF p. 399.

entities.reproduction_format: Digitized printed-volume page images with OCR text layer

entities.label: London, Lambeth Palace Library MS 487
entities.description: A late-twelfth- or early-thirteenth-century West Midlands parchment manuscript supplying Morris's numbered items I–XIX. The represented anthology spans ff. 1r–67r; its Poema Morale breaks off and is completed from Egerton MS 613.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Late twelfth or early thirteenth century

notes: The aha2688 selection uses ff. 1r–67r.

entities.label: London, British Library Cotton MS Nero A XIV
entities.description: A small parchment manuscript whose second-half-thirteenth-century Middle English section includes Ancrene Wisse and the devotional pieces Morris prints as XX–XXIII. The relevant prayers occupy current ff. 120v–131r; the current catalog describes a Latin Apostles' Creed at ff. 131r–v, creating an unresolved conflict with Morris's Middle English Lesser Creed and legacy f. 131v milestone.

date_statements.label: Relevant manuscript section
date_statements.value: Second half of the thirteenth century

notes: Target material: current ff. 120v–131r, with exact terminal Creed locus unresolved.

entities.label: London, British Library Cotton MS Vespasian A XXII
entities.description: A composite parchment codex containing the Middle English homiletic pieces Morris prints as XXIV–XXVII. Their current catalog loci are ff. 52r–56v and 57v. Morris's legacy labels are consistently two folios higher: legacy ff. 54r–59v = current ff. 52r–57v; current = legacy − 2.

date_statements.label: Composite manuscript
date_statements.value: Relevant components span the first quarter of the thirteenth through the fourteenth century

notes: The relevant pieces are cataloged at current ff. 52r–56v and 57v; the component has a Rochester Cathedral Priory provenance association.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library MS Bodley 34
entities.description: A first-half-thirteenth-century parchment manuscript, perhaps copied around 1240 in the southwest Midlands or Herefordshire, containing Sawles Warde at current ff. 72r–80v. Two leaves are missing after f. 80, so Morris supplies the ending from Royal MS 17 A XXVII. Morris's legacy locus ff. 76v–84v corresponds to the current catalog's ff. 72r–80v.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: First half of the thirteenth century, perhaps c. 1240

notes: Sawles Warde occupies current ff. 72r–80v; two leaves are missing after f. 80.

entities.label: London, British Library Royal MS 17 A XXVII
entities.description: A first-half-thirteenth-century central or West Midlands parchment manuscript containing Sawles Warde at ff. 1r–10v and an Orison of St Mary at f. 70r–v. Its Sawles Warde text supplies Morris's missing Bodley ending, carries the Johan scribal colophon and nine-line prayer at f. 10v, and serves as a collation witness throughout.

date_statements.label: Relevant manuscript sections
date_statements.value: First half of the thirteenth century

notes: Target loci: Sawles Warde ff. 1r–10v; Orison of St Mary f. 70r–v.

entities.label: London, British Library Cotton MS Titus D XVIII
entities.description: A first-half-thirteenth-century parchment manuscript whose Katherine Group component includes Sawles Warde at ff. 105v–112v, Hali Meiðhad at ff. 112v–127r, Þe Wohunge of Ure Lauerd at ff. 127r–133r, and Seinte Katerine at ff. 133v–147v. Morris transcribes the Wohunge and records selected Titus readings for Sawles Warde.

date_statements.label: Katherine Group component
date_statements.value: First half of the thirteenth century

notes: The relevant component is written in two columns; Þe Wohunge occupies ff. 127r–133r.

entities.label: London, British Library Egerton MS 613
entities.description: A composite parchment miscellany containing the first Egerton Poema Morale copy at ff. 7r–12v. Morris uses it to continue the incomplete Lambeth version in XVIII and prints the Egerton witness in Appendix I.

physical_description.extent: 74 folios plus ten modern paper endleaves at each end
notes: Target copy at ff. 7r–12v; complete digital object has 197 canvases from front cover through spine.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF object for Egerton MS 613
entities.description: A complete 197-canvas public viewer inventory from front cover through spine. The target first Egerton Poema Morale copy occupies ff. 7r–12v / canvases 39–50. The complete object is viewable but was not bulk-cached under the manifest's geographically qualified public-domain statement and linked BL terms.

notes: Canvas 1 is the front cover; canvas 197 is the spine; target ff. 7r–12v are canvases 39–50.

entities.label: George Parker's working transcript and collations for Morris's Sawles Warde
entities.description: A provisional entity for the nineteenth-century working transcript and collations that Morris explicitly credits to George Parker. It is modern editorial working material, not a medieval witness. Its survival, present location, physical extent, and exact handoff into the printed edition remain unknown.

date_statements.label: Preparation
date_statements.value: Before publication of the First Series in 1867–1868

notes: Typed as manuscript_witness only because the schema has no separate editorial-working-document class; the description and relations keep its modern status explicit.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: April 2026 duration guidance used only for the United States rights assessment of Morris's nineteenth-century publication; it is supporting legal evidence, contains no aha2688 target content, and is not part of the textual lineage.

subject.description: Encoded title page and selected Middle English source-text stream, including Appendix I and Appendix III

object.description: First Series title page and selected source-text pages
locators: numbered texts I–XXIX on odd-numbered printed pp. 3–287 / PDF pp. 71, 73, …, 355
Appendix I on printed pp. 288–295 / PDF pp. 356–363
Appendix III on printed p. 305 / PDF p. 373

scope.notes: The encoding omits the half title and front matter, all facing even-numbered modern-English translation pages, Appendix II, glossary/index/back matter, and much of the printed apparatus. Printed p. 200 / PDF p. 268 is an exceptional source-text page rather than a translation page, but it too is omitted. Printed XX and XXI are merged into XML DIV2 N=20; for XXI the subject stream begins only at Morris p. 201 / PDF p. 269 / Nero f. 124v and omits the heading and opening at p. 200 / f. 124r.

subject.description: Complete First Series image run within the larger bound scan carrier

object.description: Complete First Series edition, including preliminaries, texts, translations, appendices, glossary, and back matter

scope.notes: Local-file coverage is complete for the retained 710-page carrier. PDF pp. 399–710 are a separately bound Second Series component and are not part of this facsimile_of relation or the target edition.

subject.description: Numbered texts I–XVII, Lambeth base of XVIII, and incomplete Orison XIX

scope.notes: The Lambeth Poema Morale breaks off within XVIII; Morris continues the printed text from Egerton MS 613.

subject.description: Continuation of XVIII and parallel Moral Ode in Appendix I

scope.notes: Appendix I is a parallel witness version and does not add a second abstract Poema Morale work.

subject.description: Numbered devotional pieces XX–XXIII, including the complete Nero Orison printed as XXI

object.description: Middle English prayers and terminal Creed material
locators: Morris legacy f. 131v for the Lesser Creed, exact current locus unresolved

scope.notes: The print edition contains all of XXI beginning at p. 200 / f. 124r. Only the later U-M encoding omits that opening. The current BL catalog's Latin Apostles' Creed at ff. 131r–v conflicts with Morris's Middle English Lesser Creed and must be reconciled against images.
Foliation crosswalk: Morris legacy ff. 54r–59v = current BL ff. 52r–57v; current = legacy − 2. Individual mappings are XXIV current ff. 52r–54r, XXV ff. 54v–56r, XXVI f. 56v, and XXVII f. 57v.

subject.description: Sawles Warde text, marginal readings, and selected notes

object.description: Parker's transcript and collations as supplied to Morris

scope.notes: Morris says the accuracy of this text depends on the fidelity of Parker's transcript and collations. The working artifact itself has not been located.

object.description: Bodley Sawles Warde copy before its terminal lacuna

subject.description: Terminal supply used after the Bodley lacuna

object.description: Royal Sawles Warde ending, colophon, and prayer

subject.description: Marginal readings supplied for the printed Sawles Warde
Selected Sawles Warde collations printed in Morris's notes
Numbered text XXIX, Þe Wohunge of Ure Lauerd
Appendix III, incomplete parallel of XX On God Ureisun of Ure Lefdi
Complete 197-canvas published viewer inventory from front cover through spine

work_portion.label: aha2688 represented matter within the First Series component
locators: First Series title leaf represented by U-M: PDF pp. 7–8
Numbered source-text pages represented by U-M: odd-numbered printed pp. 3–287 = PDF pp. 71, 73, …, 355
Appendix I pages represented by U-M: printed pp. 288–295 = PDF pp. 356–363
Appendix III page represented by U-M: printed p. 305 = PDF p. 373
notes: The odd-page progression is literal and excludes the interleaved even-numbered modern-English translations. Printed p. 200 / PDF p. 268 is an exceptional source-text page opening XXI, but aha2688 omits it; the encoded complete-Orison stream resumes at printed p. 201 / PDF p. 269.

The Appendix I wrapper in the XML begins with PB N=286, but p. 286 / PDF p. 354 contains the omitted modern-English translation of XXIX; target Appendix I content begins at p. 288 / PDF p. 356. Likewise, the Appendix III wrapper begins with PB N=304, but p. 304 / PDF p. 372 belongs to omitted Appendix II; target Appendix III begins at p. 305 / PDF p. 373.
The separately bound Second Series at PDF pp. 399–710 is present in the retained carrier but is not target matter.
Coverage is complete relative to the retained 710-page PDF/carrier, not a claim that both bound series belong to the target edition.
First Series is complete on PDF pp. 5–398; Second Series preliminaries begin at PDF p. 399 and its title page is PDF p. 405.

local_copies.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, April 2026
notes: Rights-research guidance only: this PDF contains no aha2688 text, images, edition pages, or manuscript material.
The exact official provider bytes are preserved unchanged. pdfinfo reads all four pages; qpdf completes successfully with known linearization hint-table warnings.

rights.component: Checked-in XML/OCR artifact and its repository distribution
rights.basis: The CME submodule root contains Unlicense wording expressly framed around software, source code, and binaries, but the exact license coverage of CME_phase_1-2 XML/OCR data is not stated clearly enough to transfer that grant to this artifact.
notes: The public-domain status of the medieval texts and 1868 edition does not by itself determine rights in the later encoding.

rights.component: U-M CME digital text and item distribution
rights.basis: The current U-M item describes the materials as public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: The inherited XML header also preserves a generic conditional warning that copyright or distribution restrictions may apply. Both provider statements are retained rather than silently harmonized.
This item-level statement does not license modern manuscript photography held by other institutions.

rights.component: Morris's 1868 compilation, transcription, translation, notes, and apparatus
rights.basis: The completed First Series was published in 1868. The April 2026 U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A duration chart places works published before 1 January 1931 in the United States public domain.

rights.component: Internet Archive/Robarts scan files and delivery metadata
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata record supplies no rights, licenseurl, or possible_copyright_status value. The underlying 1868 edition is public domain in the United States, but that does not establish an affirmative license for every scan-layer component or provider term.

notes: This does not determine rights or contract terms for modern Lambeth photographs, scans, catalog text, or commissioned reproductions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Fragments and smaller treatises distributed across seven principal manuscript witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-nine numbered print containers arranged in witness groups, followed by appendices with parallel or supporting texts.
editorial_practices.description: Morris explicitly describes the volume as a bundle of fragments and smaller treatises. The numbered order is an editorial anthology structure, not evidence for one medieval collection or author.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete Lambeth Poema Morale, incomplete Bodley Sawles Warde, and variant Royal/Titus Sawles Warde copies
editorial_practices.representation: Egerton continues XVIII; Royal supplies the Bodley ending and marginal readings; selected Titus collations appear in notes.
editorial_practices.description: The continuous print streams are not uniformly diplomatic transcriptions of one witness. Source supplies and collations are passage-scoped and routed through the exact lineage relations.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed XVIII, XXVIII, Appendix I, and Sawles Warde apparatus

editorial_practices.source_feature: Rubricated letters in the Lambeth Poema Morale copy
editorial_practices.representation: Morris's note states that italics represent the rubrical letters of the manuscript.
editorial_practices.description: The italics at this locus encode manuscript color/rubrication, not abbreviation expansion. Their meaning must not be generalized to every italic span in the edition.
editorial_practices.scope: XVIII Poema Morale, opening line / printed p. 161

editorial_practices.source_feature: Morris First Series page images and printed layout
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR encoded to TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 with linked digital page images.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header says no OCR corrections or content editing were performed. Structural tags support discovery and page linkage but do not establish a newly checked scholarly transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete First Series with preliminaries, source texts, facing translations, notes, appendices, glossary, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Title page and Middle English source-text stream, with Appendix I and Appendix III retained and omitted sections represented only by placeholder divisions/page markers.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding omits the front matter, all modern-English translations, Appendix II tract, and back matter. It is a source-text selection, not a complete semantic encoding of the 1868 book.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Distinct print containers XX and XXI
editorial_practices.representation: Both are serialized inside one DIV2 with N=20; no DIV2 N=21 exists.
editorial_practices.description: The XML division count is therefore twenty-eight even though the edition has twenty-nine numbered print containers. Catalog parts preserve the print distinction.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Morris's complete printed XXI beginning at p. 200 / Nero f. 124r with its own heading
editorial_practices.representation: The encoded stream begins at Morris p. 201 / Nero f. 124v and continues through p. 203 / f. 126v.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M/XML representation omits the heading and complete opening page/f. 124r while preserving the remainder. This digital incompleteness is distinct from the incomplete Lambeth witness version printed as XIX.
editorial_practices.scope: BODY/DIV1[1]/DIV2[@N='20'], transition after PB N=199 to PB N=201

editorial_practices.source_feature: Morris's italic representation of rubrical letters in the opening of XVIII
editorial_practices.representation: The explanatory note survives, but the opening L element contains no HI markup distinguishing the italic letters.
editorial_practices.description: The XML alone therefore cannot identify which characters Morris printed in italics at this locus; the print facsimile is required to recover the visual distinction.
editorial_practices.scope: XVIII Poema Morale, first L element / printed p. 161

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aha2688.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for identifiers, source bibliography, 2006 U-M production declaration, uncorrected-OCR/Level-1 policy, encoded title page, omitted sections, numbered texts, page and folio milestones, appendices, notes, and selection boundaries.

evidence.citation: Structural XPath, text, page-marker, folio-marker, and hash audit of aha2688.xml.
evidence.locator: counts and per-division inspection of DIV1, DIV2, DIV3, HEAD, P, LG, L, PB, MILESTONE, NOTE, and HI
evidence.summary: Establishes 28 DIV2 elements for 29 numbered print containers, merged XX/XXI structure, omission of XXI p. 200 / f. 124r, 1,153 raw lines in 11 groups, 872 intrinsic lines in 9 groups, Appendix I and III boundaries, Lesser Creed nesting, and loss of structural italic marking at the Poema Morale rubrication note.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item AHA2688.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Official current item evidence for U-M identity, title, 1868 publication, full-text access, educational/research framing, the provider statement that materials are public domain, and the DPLA No Copyright–United States designation.

evidence.citation: Richard Morris, ed., Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises, First Series, EETS Original Series 29 and 34 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1868), in Internet Archive item oldenglishhomili00morruoft.
evidence.locator: First Series PDF pp. 5–398; title page; preface; numbered source texts on odd printed pp. 3–287; Appendix I pp. 288–295; Appendix III p. 305
evidence.summary: Immediate-edition evidence for title, imprint, two-part issue, full First Series boundary, twenty-nine print containers, translations and apparatus, source-text page ranges, appendices, complete XXI opening, and the separately bound Second Series component in the scan carrier.

evidence.citation: Richard Morris, Preface, Old English Homilies and Homiletic Treatises, First Series (1868), pp. v–xv.
evidence.summary: Morris's direct source statements identify Lambeth, Nero, Vespasian, Bodley, Royal, Titus, and Egerton dependencies; the Egerton continuation; the Royal Sawles Warde supply; Appendix III as an imperfect Royal copy of XX; Parker's transcript and collations; Royal marginal readings; Titus note collations; and confirmed versus tentative Ælfrician relationships.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for oldenglishhomili00morruoft.
evidence.summary: Provider metadata identifies the 1868 title, Robarts/University of Toronto contributor, and the exact PDF file size of 39,816,320 bytes. The rights, licenseurl, and possible_copyright_status fields are absent, so no scan-layer license is inferred.

evidence.citation: Local structural and page-sequence audit of the complete Internet Archive PDF oldenglishhomili00morruoft.pdf.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1–710; First Series pp. 5–398; Second Series binding component from p. 399, title p. 405
evidence.summary: qpdf reports no structural errors. Page count and text inspection establish a complete 710-page bound carrier, complete target First Series on pp. 5–398, and a distinct separately bound Second Series beginning at p. 399 rather than a 710-page First Series. The audited target mappings are title leaf PDF pp. 7–8; odd printed pp. 3–287 at PDF pp. 71, 73, …, 355; Appendix I printed pp. 288–295 at PDF pp. 356–363; and Appendix III p. 305 at PDF p. 373. XML wrapper PB markers 286 and 304 precede target content and belong to omitted translation/Appendix II pages.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive printed-page map and scan-data sidecar for oldenglishhomili00morruoft.
evidence.summary: Provider sidecars used with direct PDF text inspection to verify the exact printed-page-to-PDF mappings, the two-page offset before printed p. 5, and the First/Second Series binding boundary. The sidecars are linked but not cached under aha2688.

evidence.citation: The Production and Use of English Manuscripts 1060 to 1220, manuscript record for London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 487.
evidence.summary: Scholarly manuscript description for late-twelfth/early-thirteenth-century date, West Midlands localization, parchment support, 68-folio extent, approximate dimensions, and contents relevant to Morris's ff. 1r–67r selection.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library legacy LUNA link for MS 487 and current contact page.
evidence.summary: The legacy image link redirects to Verify Access, so whole-object public extent and reuse terms could not be verified. The current contact page supplies the repository enquiry route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Nero A XIV.
evidence.summary: Current institutional description for second-half-thirteenth-century date, South English origin, parchment support, 135-folio extent, dimensions, Ancrene Wisse, Middle English prayers at ff. 120v–131r, Latin Apostles' Creed at ff. 131r–v, and the currently unavailable legacy digital-content route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Vespasian A XXII.
evidence.summary: Current institutional description for the composite codex, parchment support, 129-folio extent, Rochester Cathedral Priory association, Middle English homiletic material at current ff. 52r–56v and 57v, and absence of verified current digitized content.

evidence.citation: Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Bodl. 34, manuscript_1378.
evidence.summary: Official manuscript description for first-half-thirteenth-century date, possible c. 1240 dating and southwest Midlands/Herefordshire association, 81-leaf extent, Sawles Warde at current ff. 72r–80v, and two missing leaves after f. 80.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries public TEI source for MS. Bodl. 34.
evidence.summary: Machine-readable institutional catalog evidence confirming the current foliation and Sawles Warde locus independently of Morris's legacy labels.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections copying services.
evidence.summary: Official route for mediated copying enquiries and commissioned special-collections reproductions; no public complete Bodley 34 digital object was verified.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 17 A XXVII component and parent records.
evidence.summary: Current institutional description for first-half-thirteenth-century date, central/West Midlands association, Sawles Warde ff. 1r–10v, Johan colophon at f. 10v, and Orison of St Mary f. 70r–v. Legacy full digital coverage is not currently available as a verified inventory.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Titus D XVIII, Katherine Group component.
evidence.summary: Current institutional description for the first-half-thirteenth-century parchment component, dimensions and two-column layout, Sawles Warde ff. 105v–112v, Hali Meiðhad ff. 112v–127r, Þe Wohunge ff. 127r–133r, and Seinte Katerine ff. 133v–147v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Egerton MS 613.
evidence.summary: Current institutional description for the composite manuscript, 74-folio parchment extent plus modern endleaves, approximate dimensions, and the thirteenth-century Conduct of Life / Poema Morale copy at ff. 7r–12v.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation 3 object for Egerton MS 613.
evidence.summary: Complete 197-canvas published inventory from front cover through spine; target ff. 7r–12v map to canvases 39–50. Usage text states exactly Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK, and links BL terms.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Provider terms relevant to reuse and systematic extraction of British Library-delivered content. Catalog-page CC BY statements apply to catalog descriptions, not automatically to manuscript images.

evidence.citation: British Library, digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Institutional route cited for enquiries about new digital reproductions where no current public whole-manuscript object was verified.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: Complete official four-page PDF; April 2026 revision
evidence.summary: Supporting legal evidence for the 2026 United States rule that works published before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain. The circular contains no aha2688 target content and is not a textual source.

evidence.citation: United States Code, title 17, section 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Official statutory text stating that United States copyright protection is unavailable for United States Government works, subject to the section's specified exceptions; supports the rights status of the cached Copyright Office circular itself.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The 1,211-byte root license contains Unlicense wording framed around software, source code, and binaries. It does not expressly identify the CME_phase_1-2 XML/OCR data, so the manifest does not extend it beyond its proven scope.

open_questions.question: Can complete, current, reusable whole-object facsimiles be obtained for Lambeth 487, Nero A XIV, Vespasian A XXII, Bodley 34, Royal 17 A XXVII, and Titus D XVIII?
next_steps: Use the repository-specific consultation and reproduction routes recorded in access.
Request each entire current physical object rather than target folios alone, then map aha2688 portions inside the complete delivery.
Record provider terms, shot sequence, omissions, and exact file hashes before creating any local cache bundle.

open_questions.question: How does Morris's Middle English Þe Lesse Crede and legacy f. 131v milestone correspond to the current BL catalog's Latin Apostles' Creed at ff. 131r–v?
next_steps: Inspect complete current images of Nero A XIV ff. 131r–v.
Compare the manuscript directly with Morris pp. 215–217 and the XML DIV3 creed.
Preserve any foliation, language, or item-boundary correction as an explicit crosswalk rather than replacing the legacy claim silently.

open_questions.question: Does George Parker's working Bodley transcript and Royal/Titus collation survive, and where is it held?
next_steps: Search Morris, Parker, EETS, and Oxford archival papers for the credited transcript and collations.
If located, create a separate catalog description and reproduction request rather than collapsing it into a direct Morris-to-Bodley edge.
Compare its readings with Morris's print to document the exact editorial handoff.

open_questions.question: What permission or provider-approved extraction route applies to downloading and redistributing a complete Egerton MS 613 image bundle, especially in the United Kingdom?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to clarify UK image status, systematic IIIF extraction, local research retention, and repository redistribution.
Use the public 197-canvas viewer for inspection meanwhile, without bulk-caching it.
Record any written permission and its exact geographic, commercial, attribution, and redistribution scope.

open_questions.question: Why does the U-M encoding omit Morris p. 200 / Nero f. 124r and the XXI heading while retaining pp. 201–203?
next_steps: Inspect any surviving U-M page-image inventory and OCR production logs for missing image 00000264.tif or selection rules.
Compare U-M's current downloadable source, if available, with the checked-in XML.
Keep the print edition's complete XXI separate from the digital stream's partial representation.

review_notes: Physical manuscripts, medieval textual works, nineteenth-century editorial products, digital facsimiles, U-M encoding, and repository artifact are modeled as separate layers.
Morris legacy foliation for Cotton Vespasian A XXII is two folios higher than the current BL foliation: Morris legacy ff. 54r–59v = current BL ff. 52r–57v; current = legacy − 2.
No current manuscript image was inferred from a legacy catalog statement or inaccessible viewer. Whole-object facsimile extent is distinguished from work presence and exact work loci.

The ignored cache contains two verified complete source objects: the 710-page Morris bound carrier with aha2688 present and the four-page USCO Circular 15A with target_work_presence absent. The circular supports rights analysis only and does not alter the substantive work chain.
